mnemonics: Uncaught exceptions are like uninvited guests at a JAVA party; they crash the event if you don't handle them with try-catch.
keyPoints: An uncaught exception is one that is not handled by any catch block in the call stack.
When an uncaught exception occurs, the Java runtime terminates the program and prints the exception stack trace to the console.
The default uncaught exception handler can be overridden for a thread using Thread.setUncaughtExceptionHandler().
commonMistakes: Assuming a thrown exception will be handled further up the stack without explicitly writing the code to do so.
Using empty catch blocks that swallow exceptions instead of properly handling or logging them, making debugging difficult.
